message:
fix(h2): omit SETTINGS_ENABLE_PUSH from server settings frames per RFC 7540 §6.5.2

RFC 7540 section 6.5.2 specifies that servers MUST NOT send the
SETTINGS_ENABLE_PUSH parameter in their SETTINGS frames — only clients
may advertise push willingness. Previously, to_settings() and
to_settings_minimal() unconditionally included EnablePush regardless of
the endpoint role, causing spec-non-compliant frames when serializing
server settings.

Adds role-aware variants:
- to_settings_for_role(is_client): full settings list that conditionally
  includes EnablePush only when is_client is true
- to_settings_minimal_for_role(is_client): minimal (non-default) settings
  with the same EnablePush gating

The original to_settings() and to_settings_minimal() methods remain as
client-defaulting convenience wrappers for backward compatibility.

Includes two tests verifying that server-role serialization (is_client=false)
never emits Setting::EnablePush in either the full or minimal output.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
